Hello fellow pvlib-ers,

I've found some funding to develop open source solar power forecasting tools based on pvlib python and US weather models. EPRI and Southern Company will fund a U. Arizona grad student, Derek Groenendyk, (@MoonRaker) to do the majority of the work this fall semester, though I will work closely with Derek on the project and I will maintain it in the future. The hope is that the pvlib-based forecasts can be reproducible and documentable benchmarks for the solar power forecasting community.

My plan is to add a new ``forecasting.py`` module to pvlib python, though I think we should have a conversation about if it's better to add it as a separate project under the pvlib organization. If the community is opposed to this too, then we will host it on our UA-REN organization account.

Unidata is putting together a nice project called [Siphon](https://github.com/Unidata/siphon) that makes it relatively easy to access data from a number of US weather models (though some cover more than just the US). I experimented with Siphon in [this notebook](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/wholmgren/siphon/blob/solar_testing/examples/notebooks/solar_testing.ipynb) and concluded that this project was very feasible.

One thing that we will need to do is come to some sort of agreement on how to spec out PV systems in pvlib. This has been discussed in a number of other issues in pvlib including #84 #17. So, I think this project should benefit pvlib users even if they're not interested in solar power forecasts.

Thanks in advance for your input on this. 

I'll ping a few people just to make sure that they see this... @bmu @Calama-Consulting @jforbess

issue_title: Example for how to use existing config functions
issue_body:
Related to #193 but more pragmatic. 

I typically structure my projects like this:

```
def generate_foo_config(*args, **kwargs): 
       # just manually defining foo_config['arg'] = arg
        foo_config = {....}
        return foo_config

def pipeline_foo(foo_config):
      # parse foo_config and do foo task
``` 

Where I have a pair of those methods for foo = data, train, model.  Then the main method for the whole experiment looks like this:

```
def pipeline_run(data_config, train_config, model_config):
      full_config = {
           'data': data_config,
           'train': train_config,
           'model': model_config
     }
     # load pipelined batch gen, model and run pipelined training
```

What would the best way to go about integrating this scheme with `sacred`?  There isn't a way to directly add the configs using `generate_foo_config`, right? I have to call all `generate_foo_config` with arbitrary args to get the structure of the config dicts, compose them into `full_config`, and then pass that to `experiment.add_config`? 

So something like this:

```
from experiment_pkg.train import generate_train_config, pipeline_train
from experiment_pkg.model import generate_model_config, pipeline_model
from experiment_pkg.data import generate_data_config, pipeline_batch_gen

from sacred import Experiment

experiment = Experiment('foo_bar')

full_config = {
    'data': generate_data_config(....),
    'train': generate_train_config(....),
    'model': generate_model_config(...)
}

experiment.add_config(full_config)

@experiment.automain
def main(_config):
    # do everything
```

And then the syntax for updating the hierarchical config is `experiment.run(config_updates={'train.n_epochs' = 10'})` ?

Any general insights appreciated!

issue_title: resource limit in addition to time limit
issue_body:
Hello,

This is a request for a feature that would be very important for us to use Z3 with the SPARK tools (spark-2014.org).

The idea is a command-line option which allows to specify a "resource limit" instead of (or in addition to) currently available memory and time limits. The "resource" is just some internal counter, which gets increased in Z3 on specific deterministic moments, like finding a conflict or deciding on a literal or some such. When the counter hits the specified limit, Z3 stops just as it does when it hits the time or memory limit.

Such a limit allows to get reproducible behavior of a prover even under heavy load or across machines, sometimes even across platforms. This is essential for our nightly testing, and for users of our tools, for *their* nightly testing.

The two other SMT solvers we currently use, Alt-Ergo and CVC4, both have such an option: -steps-bound for Alt-Ergo, and --rlimit for CVC4. We have worked with the two development teams to improve these options - mostly finding time-intensive modules where the internal counter was not updated, basically allowing the prover to exceed the limit.

Or maybe there already is a way in Z3 to achieve what we want?

Thanks for considering this request.

Johannes

issue_title: Z3_goal API breaks invariant I expected to hold
issue_body:
This is a minor issue.

I was playing with Z3's goal API over the weekend and I noticed that an invariant I expected to hold wasn't.

I expected that when I add a formula to a goal (via `Z3_goal_assert()`) the number of formulas in the goal (as reported by `Z3_goal_size()`) to increment by one.

This does not appear to be the case. It seems that the goal eagerly modifies inserted formulas before any tactic is applied to it.

This is what I've observed so far.

* If the inserted formula is a logical `and`  it gets split into separate formula (i.e. the goal size is incremented by `n` for a `n-ary` logical `and`, instead of by 1).
* If `true` is inserted into the goal it is ignored (i.e. increment is 0).
* If `false` is inserted into the goal all existing formulas are removed from the goal and the goal will only contain `false`. Attempting to add more formulas to this goal from now on does nothing.

From the user's perspective I wasn't expecting this to happen. I was expecting Z3 to add the formulas I asked to be inserted into the goal verbatim. I was expecting these changes to be made once a tactic was applied to the goal, not before.

Either the behaviour of `Z3_goal`s needs to be change or it should be documented what `Z3_goal_assert()` will do. My guess is changing the documentation would be the better option.

issue_title: MNIST example: Pull data from original website
issue_body:
As discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Recipes/pull/21, it's not a terribly good idea to have the MNIST example depend on the pickled MNIST dataset from deeplearning.net: (a) the website is down sometimes, causing Travis to fail for any pull requests submitted during that time, (b) asking users to download and unpickle a pickle is not a good practice ([anything could happen](http://www.cs.uic.edu/~s/musings/pickle.html) on unpickling a pickle).

We should change the MNIST example to download and load the data from Yann's original website: http://yann.lecun.com/exdb/mnist/
It's relatively easy to read using a combination of `gzip.open`, `np.fromfile` and `np.reshape`, you just need the correct dtype, offset (to skip the header) and shape.

I also wrote a generic MNIST format reader some time which can be used as a reference (it can be simplified a lot since we can just hard-code the shape and dtype):
```python
def read_mnistfile(fn):
    with gzip.open(fn, 'rb') as f:
        # read header
        zeros = struct.unpack("<H", f.read(2))
        magic, ndim = struct.unpack("<2B", f.read(2))
        # read matrix shape
        shape = list(struct.unpack(">%dL" % ndim, f.read(ndim * 4)))
        # create matrix
        types = {\
                0x08: np.uint8,
                0x09: np.int8,
                0x0B: np.int16,
                0x0C: np.int32,
                0x0D: np.float32,
                0x0E: np.float64
        }
        dtype = types.get(magic, None)
        if dtype is None:
            raise ValueError("This is not a valid MNIST matrix file.")
        mat = np.frombuffer(f.read(), dtype).reshape(shape)
    return mat
```

issue_title: Implementation of Gazebo in ROS with DroneKit-SITL
issue_body:
DroneKit provides such a modular and powerful platform for building of UAVs. With DroneKit-SITL, 2D simulation can help developers to clearly visualise the behaviour of the vehicle, and will tend to succeed with extensive error checking in the script. 

However, the problem arises with the vehicle flying indoor. A 2 dimensional simulation is no longer sufficient, and the application demands for more extensive simulation in 3D using Gazebo, for instance. 

It will be relatively useful to be able to CAD a virtual environment for indoor flying, which will be very useful for applications such as: 

1. Navigating in a tunnel with constant wind disturbances 
2. Flying indoor for SLAM 
3. Obstacle Avoidance 
4. Visualisation of computer vision related work 

and the list goes on and on. 

Does the team behind this open-sourced platform has any intention of implementing the Gazebo simulator? O

#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

import os
from satpy import Scene
from pyresample import utils
#from satpy.utils import debug_on
#debug_on()
BASEDIR = "C:\scrypts\K7"
FILES = os.path.join(BASEDIR, "MOD021KM.A2018175.0955.061.2018175194932.hdf")
print(FILES)
global_scene = Scene(filenames = [FILES])
print(global_scene.available_dataset_names())

#### Problem description

I want to look at weather satellite data over Svalbard and downloaded Terra MODIS data from LAADS, I am unable to create a Scene with the data though. It seems satpy is unable to find a reader for the data, but on satpy's readthedocs page (https://satpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) on the bottom table it says that satpy should have a reader for this type of data. I tried manually setting the reader with kwarg: reader ="hdfeos_l1b", but then I get:  "ValueError: No supported files found". 

I also had some difficulty creating a scene with NOAA-15 and NOAA-18 data, maybe I should open up a different issue for that problem? 

#### Expected Output

I want to load up the data and get a printout of the available channels.

#### Actual Result, Traceback if applicable

No handlers could be found for logger "satpy.readers.yaml_reader"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "aq.py", line 9, in <module>
    global_scene = Scene(filenames = [FILES])
  File "C:\Users\magnus\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\satpy\scene.py", 
line 143, in __init__
    reader_kwargs=reader_kwargs)
  File "C:\Users\magnus\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\satpy\scene.py", 
line 188, in create_reader_instances
    ppp_config_dir=self.ppp_config_dir)
  File "C:\Users\magnus\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\satpy\readers\__init__.py",
line 403, in load_readers
    reader_instance = load_reader(reader_configs, **reader_kwargs)
  File "C:\Users\magnus\Anaconda2\lib\site-packages\satpy\readers\__init__.py",
line 259, in load_reader
    **reader_kwargs
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not callable

#### Versions of Python, package at hand and relevant dependencies

OS: Windows 7
SatPy Version: 0.8.4
PyResample Version: 1.9.3
Python version: 2.7

####

Since 1.9.3 was relased some test can break in django 1.11:
 
`def _patch_settings(self):
        '''
            Patches settings to add support for django-webtest authorization
            and (optional) to disable CSRF checks.
            '''
        self._DEBUG_PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS = settings.DEBUG_PROPAGATE_EXCEPTIONS
>       self._MIDDLEWARE = self.settings_middleware[:]
E       TypeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute '__getitem__'
`

The context is a django application compatible with 1.11 but whose settings still uses the MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES setting.

This commit broke it : https://github.com/django-webtest/django-webtest/pull/82/commits/7c8b821c135281cf76020e9ab1455b460516bb3b

I don't fully understand the purpose of this commit since by default, whatever the settings are,
hasattr(settings, 'MIDDLEWARE') will return True.

It's seems to me that this commit basically dropped the  MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES support.

issue_title: Investigate the use of ordered dictionary for schema attributes
issue_body:
As mentioned in the developer meeting on 03/12/2015 I've spotted a problem in Core due to initializing objects can potentially require that other schema attributes be correctly set for a parent object.

I'd like to investigate changing the attributes in the Schema to be stored in an ordered dictionary as of 6.1.15.
The default behaviour would be to have an ordered dictionary but to allow for unmodified classes to still use a dictionary style definition of the schema attributes.

This shouldn't be too invasive but I'd rather not introduce this into 6.1.14.

I have a checker that fails with 6.1.14 because the metadata per job is missing. The strange thing is that the combined metadata for the master job is there. 
```

On a side note, subjobs are not returned as proxy objects, so it is annoying to work interactively with them.

issue_title: 32K (!) files in the gangadir/shared/<user_name>/ directory cause file system error on AFS
issue_body:
AFS has a limit of 64K file names per directory. This limit is reduced depending on the length of the file names, it is reduced to less than 22K if the file names are longer than 33 letters.

https://lists.openafs.org/pipermail/openafs-info/2002-September/005812.html

My `gangadir/shared/koschube/` directory contains `31 707` files currently, and I appear to have hit the limit.

It is no longer possible for me to create new jobs in ganga, I am getting errors like:

```
OSError: [Errno 27] File too large: '/afs/cern.ch/user/k/koschube/workspace/gangadir2/shared/koschube/conf-1e3fe559-6b5e-4db3-a9cb-25912de073ed'`
```

Please note that I had started with a completely new gangadir just a few weeks ago and that I have submitted less than 100 jobs in total (!). So this definitely isn't caused by some outrageous use case.

issue_title: We should consider hidding resources permissions when we only have the read permission
issue_body:
This is related to https://github.com/mozilla-services/kinto/pull/88

When we have the ``read`` permission to list existing buckets, we can ask for a bucket and in that case we can list its permissions. I think it is only something that somebody that can modify this permission should be able to see.

should

- [ ] may be turn on `openmp` for pytraj as default.

- [ ] check cpptraj's `config.h` if `openmp` was used

- [ ]  may be try linking to cpptraj with openmp flag to check if cpptraj was installed with openmp?

- [ ]  should check cpptraj version in Version.h to avoid wasting compiling cython files if version is too old.

adapted from http://sahandsaba.com/thirty-python-language-features-and-tricks-you-may-not-know.html

and other sources and my experience.

issue_title: featurize_dataframe mishandles features that are equally sized numpy arrays
issue_body:
When BaseFeaturizer.featurize_dataframe() is called for a featurizer which returns equal-dimension
numpy arrays (such as OrbitalFieldMatrix and the in-dev ManyBodyTensor), the code exits in error,
as shown in the Python code and output attached in testfeat.txt. The reason for this is that featurize_dataframe joins the features into a numpy array, which is interpreted as a multidimensional array if and only if each element of the array has the same length. If the features are single values, the array is 1D, and if they are variable-length arrays, the array is interpreted as a 1D collection of other objects. The `assign` call in `featurize_dataframe` does not handle multidimensional arrays.

A possible fix is shown in testfeat.py, in which a dataframe is featurized by adding a Series initialized
from a list of features. As shown in testnew.txt, there does not seem to be a significant speed difference between these methods. I apologize if the fix is underthought; I am not familiar with the reasoning for the original design, so I may well be missing something.

[testfeat.txt](https://github.com/hackingmaterials/matminer/files/1594941/testfeat.txt)
[testnew.txt](https://github.com/hackingmaterials/matminer/files/1594942/testnew.txt)

issue_title: Test mtime is copied in CopyDirectoryStep
issue_body:
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2783

1. Create repo
`pulp-admin rpm repo create --repo-id=test --feed=https://bizhang.fedorapeople.org/testrepo/`

2. Set generate-sqlite to true
`pulp-admin rpm repo update  --generate-sqlite=true --repo-id=test`

3. Sync repo
`pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id=test`

4. check mtime for the sqlite files in `/var/lib/pulp/published/yum/https/repos/test/repodata`

5. Upload a rpm
`pulp-admin rpm repo uploads rpm --file=tiger-1.0-4.noarch.rpm --repo-id=test`

6. Sync again 
`pulp-admin rpm repo sync run --repo-id=test`

7. check mtime for old sqlite files in `/var/lib/pulp/published/yum/https/repos/test/repodata` remain the same.

issue_title: Error in classifying multi class documents
issue_body:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to follow the example at https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/examples/nlp/dynamic_lstm.py

I have documents stored in the directory ``text``, each file corresponds to one document, named ``1``, ``2`` ... There are 10 000 files.

I have a label file named ``labels``, contains 10 000 lines, each line is a label of each file. 

There are 6 labels, ``a``, ``b`` ... ``f``.

Here is my code:

```{python}
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

import tflearn
from tflearn.data_utils import to_categorical, pad_sequences

import csv
import numpy as np
from sklearn import metrics, cross_validation

import tensorflow as tf

import random
import time

import string

import getopt
import sys
import os

data_dir = "text"   
model_size = 2000  
nb_epochs      = 50   
label_file = "labels"
MAX_FILE_ID = 50000

### Training data

labels = ["a","b","c","d","e","f"]

print('Read labels')

def load_label (label_file):
    with open (label_file) as f:
        return f.read().splitlines()

Y = load_label(label_file)

for i in range(len(Y)):
    Y[i] = labels.index(Y[i])

print('Read content')

def load_content (file_name):
    with open(file_name) as f:
        return f.read()

X = []
for i in range (MAX_FILE_ID):
    file_name = data_dir + '/' + str(i + 1)
    if os.path.isfile (file_name):
        X.append (load_content(file_name)) 

X_train, X_test, Y_train, Y_test = cross_validation.train_test_split(X, Y,
    test_size=0.2, random_state=2017)

Y_train = to_categorical (Y_train, nb_classes = len(labels))
Y_test = to_categorical (Y_test, nb_classes = len(labels))

### Process vocabulary

print('Process vocabulary')

vocab_processor = tflearn.data_utils.VocabularyProcessor(max_document_length = model_size, min_frequency = 0)
X_train = np.array(list(vocab_processor.fit_transform(X_train)))
X_test = np.array(list(vocab_processor.fit_transform(X_test)))

X_train = pad_sequences(X_train, maxlen=model_size, value=0.)
X_test = pad_sequences(X_test, maxlen=model_size, value=0.)

n_words = len(vocab_processor.vocabulary_)
print('Total words: %d' % n_words)

### Models

print('Build model')

net = tflearn.input_data([None, model_size])
net = tflearn.embedding(net, input_dim=model_size, output_dim=128)
net = tflearn.lstm(net, 128, dropout=0.5)
net = tflearn.fully_connected(net, n_units = len(labels) , activation='softmax')
net = tflearn.regression(net, optimizer='adam', learning_rate=0.001,
                         loss='categorical_crossentropy')

print ('Train model')

model = tflearn.DNN(net, tensorboard_verbose=3)

print ('Predict')
model.fit(X_train, Y_train, validation_set=(X_test, Y_test), show_metric=True,
          batch_size=32)
```

and I met an error

``InvalidArgumentError (see above for traceback): indices[0,0] = 4683 is not in [0, 2000)``

issue_title: Questions on fasttext supervised - char ngram functionality, vector representation of labels

Hi, 
Apologies if these questions are repeats or just plain dumb.
1) 
My first question relates to this:
https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/issues/46
I am wondering whether/when the character ngram functionality might be added to the supervised model - in the above post you say you are working on adding those functionalities. Am I right that they are still not present, and when might they appear? (I have a specific data set for which I think they could be very valuable).
2) 
My second question relates to this:
https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/issues/169
I would like to get some idea of the relationship of my labels to each other - I have a large amount, the labels are somewhat fuzzy, and I would like to analyze them as well as use them for classification.

Thanks!

issue_title: Use CERTBOT_DOCS for Apache CLI defaults
issue_body:
Certbot often tries to determine the underlying OS and changes its default behavior and config values to match. These defaults often show up in our documentation though which means that the documentation changes based on who built it.

In #6120, we introduced the environment variable `CERTBOT_DOCS` to avoid this behavior and print the same defaults in our documentation regardless of the system it is run on. We added this to nginx in #6137. We don't do this in Apache though as can be seen at https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/6232#discussion_r203197775.

We should respect `CERTBOT_DOCS` in the Apache plugin.

cc @joohoi

## My operating system is (include version):
ubuntu 14.04

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
certbot-auto 0.14.0

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
In cron `PATH=$PATH:/usr/sbin /root/certbot-auto renew --apache --quiet --no-self-upgrade --post-hook "service apache2 reload"`
```
Encountered vhost ambiguity but unable to ask for user guidance in non-interactive mode. Currently Certbot needs each vhost to be in its own conf file, and may need vhosts to be explicitly labelled with ServerName or ServerAlias directories.
Falling back to default vhost *:443...
Encountered vhost ambiguity but unable to ask for user guidance in non-interactive mode. Currently Certbot needs each vhost to be in its own conf file, and may need vhosts to be explicitly labelled with ServerName or ServerAlias directories.
Falling back to default vhost *:443...
...
```

Also, after this ran apache was not running.

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
If the config files are OK before running certbot renew: Renewing certificates should not cause Apache to stop, no matter whether successful or not.
Also, I don't have any apache config files with multiple virtualhost directives. So I don't see why it would complain.

issue_body:
If we don't know how to bootstrap Certbot for your operating system in `certbot-auto`, we print:
```
Sorry, I don't know how to bootstrap Certbot on your operating system!

You will need to install OS dependencies, configure virtualenv, and run pip install manually.
Please see https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/contributing.html#prerequisites
for more info.
```
However, the list there is hardly complete. We should do better.

issue_title: renew doesn't print a timestamp for logging purposes

My crontab contains:

    30 2 * * 1 /opt/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto renew >> /var/log/le-renew.log

issue_title: Allow unknown uncompressed data size in lz4.block.decompress()
issue_body:
In some cases you get lz4 compressed chunk of data without knowing uncompressed data size.

I suggest to allow uncompressed_size (parameter of lz4.block.decompress) accept zero value to indicate that data size is unknown.

    x = 1  # my comment

I tried

    import tokenize
    atok.find_token(node.last_token, tokenize.COMMENT)

where `node` is an AST node e.g. an AST Name node for 'x'

The find never works.  Looking at the source code of asttokens, I think it is because when `find_token()` calls `next_token()` to iterate through the tokens, it never passes through True to the `include_extra` parameter of `next_token()`.

issue_title: zhmccli: Support for 'authorization controls' of a Partition
issue_body:
### Actual behavior
Following Partition Controls properties can not be said with the CLI:
permit-cross-partition-commands
access-basic-counter-set
access-basic-sampling     
access-coprocessor-group-set
access-crypto-activity-counter-set   
access-diagnostic-sampling          
access-extended-counter-set         
access-global-performance-data       
access-problem-state-counter-set

### Expected behavior
It should be possible to set the 'authorization controls' as options during the 'partition created' or 'partition update'

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.14.0

As it stands, we have a Pulumi-specific backend checked into this repo.  That's probably the wrong way to factor this for a truly vendor agnostic open source project.

One idea I had here is to take the backend interface one step further, and make it a gRPC plugin interface, and to introduce a new plugin type, `cloud`.  This way we can distribute the Pulumi cloud plugin out of band.  For instance, perhaps you'd install it as follows:

```bash
$ pulumi plugin install cloud pulumi
```

This would also help to facilitate new implementations of the backend, such as the one we envision being run inside of a Kubernetes cluster someday.

issue_title: `pulumi new` should fail if the directory isn't empty
issue_body: After discussion with @lukehoban: We should make `pulumi new` return an error if the directory isn't empty, and output instructions to create the directory and cd into it. This aligns with what some other similar tools do and allows us to get rid of the `mkdir <project> && cd <project>` instructions before all our `pulumi new` tutorials, as anyone who runs `pulumi new` in a non-empty directory will be forced to create a new directory in order to proceed.

issue_title: GNU extensions used in call to strftime break non-glibc systems
issue_body:
This line contains a GNU extension in the format string passed to `strftime` (specifically the `%-d` part). This causes the `timestamp` variable to be an empty string on non-glibc systems (like the Alpine Linux Docker images, which use musl libc).

Removing the hyphen modifier from `%-d` should be painless on glibc and non-glibc systems alike. Stripping leading zeros does not appear to be required by [RFC2616](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2616#section-3.3) or [RFC822](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc822#section-5) (which seems to be the format used here). Could this possibly be removed for good?

https://github.com/capless/warrant/blob/3d6aa8c84841a1c7183f702ed7b4ad993c1ff37a/warrant/aws_srp.py#L170

I have been bugged by some "stylistically strange" parts on the code. Personally, I like to follow, for the most part, pep8. The line length is a different topic (see below), but without considering that, a call to `pep8 --ignore=E501 ./django-helpdesk` yields 872 lines, of a total of 36 different complains.

Regarding the long lines, a quick look at them gives:
- 1974 lines longer than 79 characters
- 982 lines longer than 99 characters
- 414 lines longer than 119 characters
- 220 lines longer than 139 characters

Long lines are counted with multiplicity.

There are a lot of long lines in `migrations` and also `south_migrations`. Maybe in the branch 0.2 the migrations can be squashed and cleaned up... ok, that is a different topic. But note that without both migration folders, there are 831 non-length issues and 449 long lines (>79).

My question would be, it is a good idea to bring a major clean-up? Do you feel that it is worth to follow pep8 (from now on)? Or you feel that it is better to go beyond that and use `pylint`?

It is a tedious task, but it is quite straightforward. I don't mind to prepare a PR for that, but I am not sure if you agree on that topic, or maybe prefer to follow a subset of the `pep8` may be followed, or go all out with `pylint`.

issue_title: Error during WebSocket handshake: Sent non-empty 'Sec-WebSocket-Protocol' header but no response was received
issue_body:
The WebSockify bridge of noVNC seems to be broken since commit 4099949984eb80ef33c2d0dd216991124975a5d2.

The error that appears in Chrome's console is:

```js
websock.js:278 WebSocket connection to 'wss://janitor.technology/websockify' failed: Error during WebSocket handshake: Sent non-empty 'Sec-WebSocket-Protocol' header but no response was received
```

I had a look at the headers received from WebSockify's server, and indeed it seems that `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` is no longer being sent.

Before 4099949984eb80ef33c2d0dd216991124975a5d2:

```
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Server: WebSockify Python/2.7.12
Date: Tue, 07 Feb 2017 15:52:33 GMT
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: yHBB/hXTWGaTjk29VQhWk2not24=
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: binary
```

After 4099949984eb80ef33c2d0dd216991124975a5d2:

```
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: ZefPJQjbgtmJGvzKFp7JvLWpDvI=
```

Maybe it could help to continue sending `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: binary` in the WebSocket headers? Or am I doing something wrong here?

First off, thank you for this project! Second, have read [https://github.com/novnc/websockify/wiki/Token-based-target-selection](https://github.com/novnc/websockify/wiki/Token-based-target-selection) and third, have tried + read [wstelnet.js](https://github.com/novnc/websockify/blob/master/include/wstelnet.js) ....

As per issue https://github.com/novnc/websockify/issues/213 is it incumbent upon user to rewrite wstelnet.js to support the token path syntax (as from looking at telnet code and also trying, it seems like the URI is not rebuilt/supported (only host and port lines 147-168 of [wstelnet.js](https://github.com/novnc/websockify/blob/master/include/wstelnet.js), or am I missing something in front of my nose?)

Currently I have noVNC working with tokens/websockify but am confused about whether tokens should work with wstelnet.js?   Please feel free to point me elsewhere and/or close if wrong forum/medium.

issue_title: Ingest should report product changes differences when detected
issue_body:
When running `datacube ingest` on a product that already exists, code verifies that the destination product matches the definition supplied in the ingestor config. If the two are not exactly the same `datacube ingest` gives up with an error

```
Ingest config differs from the existing output product, 
but allow_product_changes=False 
```

and gives no indication to the user as to what changes were detected. Actual changes are actually available to ingestor code already:

https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/b987f362ed693408fd746fe8dcf5b0995a44c8fc/datacube/scripts/ingest.py#L140-L146

Changes are returned and are classified into `safe` and `unsafe` categories, displaying those to the user would be super useful.

issue_title: Sparql language comparison doesn't matc '' to None
issue_body:
jus...@onebrightspace.com, 2009-09-01T09:44:13.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. Add triple ?abc with no language
2. Sparql query: FILTER (lang(?abc) = '')

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
Expected: Match
Actual: No Match

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
2.4.1

Please provide any additional information below.
All triples without a language have a defualt of None.  I would expect an
empty quote to match a None language.  So I can say:

FILTER (lang(?abc) = 'en-gb' || lang(?abc) = '')

Fix:

--- ./rdflib/sparql/sparqlOperators.py
+++ ./rdflib/sparql/sparqlOperators.py
@@ -313,7 +313,7 @@
         if v == None: return ""
         try :
             val = bindings[v]
-            if val == None:
+            if val == None or val.language == None:
                 return ""
             else :
                 return val.language
